evals.prompt: That competitive analysis session is wrapped up (full transcript is in transcript-1.md). Before we finish — run a session retrospective on it and log it so the team can review AI-collaboration patterns later. There's no existing retro log yet.
evals.expected_output: Creates docs/ai-retros/entries/<session-date>-<slug>.md containing one schema-compliant v1.2 entry: evidence-cited friction (audience/length not specified until turn 9, missing analytics access, stale Retrium pricing as agent-error), every friction item labeled from the fixed 10-label vocabulary AND ending with a '→ Fix:' line, a 'Do this first' section with exactly one fix, and no praise/filler. Entry is committed.

evals.prompt: Session's done — transcript-2.md has the full transcript of today's webinar email session. Run a retro and add it to our existing AI retro log (docs/ai-retros/entries/ in this repo). Please make sure past entries stay intact.
evals.expected_output: Creates a new entry file without modifying the two existing v1.1-stamped entries; entry cites the merge-tag guess and missing Customer.io access with fixes; reads the most recent prior entry's 'One change' (legacy v1.1 section) and notes that confirming sequence structure upfront was adopted this session.

evals.prompt: Can you create a retrospective for the design team for Friday? Start-stop-continue format, and add an icebreaker.
evals.expected_output: Does NOT invoke ai-session-retro and does NOT write anything to docs/ai-retros/. Treats this as a product/facilitation request (e.g. TeamRetro retrospective creation or a clarifying question) — the skill's exclusion rule for retrospective-product work is respected.

evals.prompt: Here's a transcript of an AI session from three weeks ago (transcript-1.md, session ran 2026-06-20). Backfill a retro entry for it into docs/ai-retros/entries/ — we're seeding the log with past sessions.
evals.expected_output: Entry is dated 2026-06-20 (the session date, not today), filename starts 2026-06-20, header includes '**Backfilled:** yes', and the Step 4 close-the-loop check is skipped (no adoption claims about other entries).
